raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year> I bought tickets to an event for {$73.00}. The event has a no refund policy for inclement weather or any other reason for not attending. There was inclement weather that day so I decided to go anyway. We got there, and none of the services they were supposed to provide were open ( no photo ops, no shows, most rides were closed ). Then they kicked us out early and cancelled the event. Of course it was in person, so I have nothing in writing. Except my emails to contact them ( no response ), and extensive reviews online of other patrons angry for the same reason. I opened a dispute with AMEX. It was denied. I opened it again. Also denied. And the customer service agent on the phone this last time was extremely rude, and completely unhelpful. I pay a decent amount for that card, and theyre supposed to help the customer. Not other businesses. I was told since i have nothing in writing from the merchant about cancellation, they wont refund me. That seems illegal given Im disputing the services received. Since I was unsatisfied with them. How can I fight this?
